title: The Giant Hand Zero versus Sho
display_title: The Giant Hand Zero versus Sho
wikitext:
[]: Post by V » Sun Feb 10, 2008 3:30 pm reaching for Shinji is Zero and not Sho.

[]: Post by UrsusArctos » Sun Feb 10, 2008 8:20 pm Have you forgotten, "Rei point Rei Rei Rei Rei Rei Rei Rei Rei Ich Pasento"? I might be missing a "Rei" there, but doesn't that seem to be a stretch, Ritsuko saying the word "Rei" when part of Rei herself (The Rei in Eva-00) is right there? And have you forgotten the big fuss they make when the right hand wrenches free of the bindings to protect Shinji? That's an outstretched right hand over there. It can't have moved just like that. See the way the left hand is level at the Eva's side? The right hand also rose from being at the side to protect Shinji. Eva-00's right hand may not be clenched in a fist...the Eva might have opened her fist at the last moment. Also note the little bit of red near the right wrist...somewhat like pink cooling fluid over orange. She simply punched through the wall to the cooling fluid inside it. The fist is not Eva-01, it's Eva-00. QED.

[]: Post by Sachi » Sun Feb 10, 2008 8:33 pm I just thought it was an ironic shot. Shinji was too busy reading the "WELCOME TO NERV" pamphlet that he didn't notice the hand. Which led me to assume that it was Unit-01. That makes sense that it could be 00 though. I never over analyze this much...

[]: Post by Reichu » Sun Feb 10, 2008 9:10 pm It's Eva-00. This one really isn't THAT hard. Just let it rest, V.

[]: Post by Ornette » Sun Feb 10, 2008 10:01 pm It is inconceivable that it is Eva-01's hand. Both arms were at the Eva's side. The Eva was under restraints, it still had the termination plug in it. If it were to move at all, alarms would have gone off. Ritsuko would be going "but that's impossible". There's no way that the people in the cage would fix the restraints by the time Ritsuko got there and then not let her know that something impossible just happened while she was on her way there. Being that it isn't Eva-01's hand, it's possible that it was some other Eva's hand, perhaps one of the failed prototypes. But those are down below, and they were on their way to the cages. The best option left is Eva-00.

[]: Post by V » Sun Feb 10, 2008 10:26 pm She simply punched through the wall to the cooling fluid inside it.

[]: Post by ZapX » Sun Feb 10, 2008 10:55 pm lolWUT?????!!!!

[]: Post by Sachi » Sun Feb 10, 2008 10:58 pm It's actually Chuck Norris's 3rd fist that came out of his beard. PROBLEM SOLVED.

[]: Post by Ornette » Sun Feb 10, 2008 10:59 pm V wrote: No I don't think Eva-01's hands have moved; I think the animators made a mistake and they shouldn't have animated Eva-01's hand like that only to later show it restrained; by all means it is a mistake but it was meant to be Eva-01.

[]: Post by V » Sun Feb 10, 2008 11:11 pm Ornette wrote: You think that it is supposed to be Eva-01's hand because you don't believe they should show something that has to do with an Eva that isn't introduced yet?

[]: Post by Ornette » Mon Feb 11, 2008 12:23 am V wrote: Actually I wouldn't put that past them, yeah. Just that I don't think Zero's fist went all the way through the wall (unless repeat viewing proves me wrong, but it doesn't appear to be so...)

[]: Post by Reichu » Mon Feb 11, 2008 1:03 am Since I happened to be making it at the same time, a higher-quality composite . (Well, okay, it's not quite the whole image; maybe I'll finish it later...) In addition, the storyboards explicitly state that this is Eva-00's hand. The End.

[]: Post by UrsusArctos » Mon Feb 11, 2008 2:17 am Reichu wrote: In addition, the storyboards explicitly state that this is Eva-00's hand.

[]: Post by Eva Yojimbo » Mon Feb 11, 2008 8:28 am I must say that in retrospect it seems obvious it's Zero's hand, this is one particular foreshadowing that wasn't handled well at all. Why do you show a hand reaching for Shinji bathed in purple and have it NOT be Sho? Then only to later, in the NEXT episode, show the Eva the hand belongs to in a context where it would be impossible to connect the two? I mean, barring the "restraints" thingy, I think many would connect the hand with Sho first-hand. Anyway, I just think this is a great element in the series that was handled rather poorly.

[]: Post by Zuggy » Mon Feb 11, 2008 8:42 am V wrote: This isn't Eva-00's right hand.

[]: Post by TemporalKnight » Mon Feb 11, 2008 9:13 am Holy sh!t! When did this happen?! I never even thought it worth looking at something that was so obviously just Sho's hand and Shinji being too preoccupied to notice quite literally the elephant in the room! But no, those pictures do seem to make it quite clear that it is Unit-00. Wow, you really do learn something new everyday.

[]: Post by V » Mon Feb 11, 2008 10:25 am UrsusArctos wrote: Reichu wrote: In addition, the storyboards explicitly state that this is Eva-00's hand.

[]: Post by Ornette » Mon Feb 11, 2008 10:29 am Eva Yojimbo wrote: Why do you show a hand reaching for Shinji bathed in purple and have it NOT be Sho? Then only to later, in the NEXT episode, show the Eva the hand belongs to in a context where it would be impossible to connect the two? I mean, barring the "restraints" thingy, I think many would connect the hand with Sho first-hand. Anyway, I just think this is a great element in the series that was handled rather poorly.

[]: Post by Eva Yojimbo » Mon Feb 11, 2008 10:55 am Ornette wrote: I never thought that it was Sho's hand,

[]: Post by Ornette » Mon Feb 11, 2008 11:09 am Eva Yojimbo wrote: 1. The purple hue of the scene suggests Sho. Further, the purple "coolant" as V calls it is surrounding Sho, not Zero. 2. Yui reaching for Shinji makes more sense in terms of imagery than Zero reaching for Shinji.
